buyer_dependent_items.text: John Deere to share the outcome of its priority re-assessment and second-provider evaluation, and whether the POC resumes

competitive_position.summary: Recency-weighted, the live threat is the unnamed external I/O provider John Deere began deep-diving on 11 May 2026 (it caused the POC pause), alongside John Deere's own in-house build that Eduardo confirmed made 'tremendous progress' in 2025. GEP is the dated peer threat in spend analysis. Oracle and SAP Ariba are logged in Salesforce but absent from all recent calls, so they rank low/dormant; Coupa was only a rep hypothetical and has faded.

competitors.name: Unnamed external I/O provider
competitors.quote: Talked with Karen on May 11th - doing a deep dive on another external I/O provider. Did not indicate which one.
competitors.how_we_win: On the 20 Jul reconnect, learn who and why, then reframe Zycus as the agentic accelerator their evaluation lacks and restart a tightly-scoped POC.

competitors.name: In-house build / status quo (SAP-UI engine, iProcure, OpenAI/Ultrametrics/Worldlink partnerships)
competitors.quote: 2025 has seen tremendous progress... the team has a clear, very good vision of where we are going, and for me Zycus is that potential partner to accelerate what we don't have in our portfolio.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Merlin intake plus autonomous negotiation as modular accelerants over their SAP build; make continued pure-build's cost and timeline explicit versus faster realized value.

competitors.name: GEP
competitors.quote: Three-Prong Approach: a. Pilot Spend Analysis to counter GEP
competitors.how_we_win: Run a free spend-analysis proof of value on a 3-month data slice to demonstrate insight-to-action superiority.

competitors.name: Oracle
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with AI/agentic depth (32 AI patents) and configurability versus legacy suite rigidity.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.how_we_win: Differentiate on Merlin intake and autonomous negotiation; note John Deere's own SAP IT team will view Zycus as competition, so anchor on complement-not-replace.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: let's say we're replacing a Coupa shop, they're already fairly mature
competitors.source: Avoma proposal review 18 Dec 2025 (rep hypothetical only)
competitors.how_we_win: Not in active evaluation; awareness only, no action needed.

champion_strength.source: Avoma 1:1 30 Sep 2025; Avoma demo 24 Jun 2025; SF contact roles
champion_strength.summary: Engagement is real but no single power-champion is driving the deal for Zycus. Eduardo Viera is the strongest advocate (primary Executive Sponsor) but conditional on build-versus-buy fit and not pushing internally; Karen Powers is enthusiastic but lacks budget authority; Karen Pagorek owns the day-to-day but is the one who paused the POC.
champion_strength.champion: Eduardo Viera (developing); Karen Powers (enthusiastic, no budget power)

meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: Two economic-buyer candidates are mapped in Salesforce but neither has committed budget on a call. Eduardo Viera (Global Director, Supply Management Indirect Materials and Services, primary contact and Executive Sponsor) controlled the make-versus-buy and partnership strategy on 30 Sep 2025, framing his stance as 'if there is a solution working already in the marketplace that we don't need to reinvent, let's try to see how we can adapt that,' but his sponsorship is conditional on fit and he has not driven the deal internally. Joshua Jepsen (SVP and Chief Financial Officer, Executive Sponsor) is the ultimate budget authority for the $450k but has never appeared on any call or activity. Directly re-engaging Eduardo to broker access to Jepsen, and confirming who signs, is the key move.

recommended_moves: Run the 20 Jul reconnect with Karen Pagorek to diagnose the POC halt and name the rival provider.
Deliver the John Deere-specific value-engineering benchmark modeling ROI against their own baseline metrics.
Re-engage Eduardo Viera to broker a power sponsor and a path to CFO Joshua Jepsen.
